title: Rural People and Communities in the 21st Century: Resilience and Transformation By David L. Brown and Kai A. Schafft Polity. 2011. 224 pages. $69.95 cloth, $36.95 paper
abstract: The changes experienced in rural communities in recent decades have been profound and are ongoing. Effects of economic restructuring, urbanization, demographic shifts into and out of rural places, metropolitan expansion, environmental changes and rapid technological change have altered the economies, social life and cultures of rural places. Focusing on the United States and with some discussion of Europe, Brown and Schaaft provide an overview of the social, economic and to a lesser extent cultural changes affecting rural people and places. The authors provide a clear and systematic approach to chronicling the conceptual frameworks through which social scientists have grappled with understanding the complex interconnecting factors that are shaping patterns of change in rural places the world over. Building on well-developed data about rurality in relation to metropolitan society, urbanization and population redistribution, changes in community social structure, institutions and natural resource extraction, and changes in characteristics of rural populations, rural economy and socioeconomic well-being, they launch their call for an integrated, sustainability driven rural development policy for the United States.
